caption_rule: A photo may show visible equipment or setting, but cannot identify hidden workloads, model training, or inference service without same-site evidence.
Do not infer EUV allocation, wafer starts, yield, node, packaging capacity, HBM supply, or AI-accelerator volume from a generic manufacturing image.
Do not count GPUs, accelerators, power draw, utilization, or training capacity unless the image and caption are tied to a sourced inventory.
Grid imagery illustrates interconnection and transmission concepts, not available megawatts, queue position, reliability, or fuel mix.
A turbine photo does not prove runtime, fuel contract, emissions rate, delivered capacity, or data-center service.
Batteries should be captioned as one grid component, not as proof that storage solves AI data-center power constraints.
A nuclear or cooling-tower image does not prove a PPA delivered power, an SMR operates, or a data center is supplied by that plant.
A license candidate is not publication-ready until exact license, creator, attribution, file revision, and commercial-use compatibility are recorded.
